echo "Locate function and suspicious indexing sites..." && \
grep -n "isGatherShuffledSingleRegisterEntry" -n llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp && \
FUNC_LINE=$(nl -ba llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp | grep -n "isGatherShuffledSingleRegisterEntry(" | head -n1 | cut -d: -f1) && \
START=$((FUNC_LINE-30)) && END=$((FUNC_LINE+300)) && \
echo "Showing context lines $START-$END" && sed -n "${START},${END}p" llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp | nl -ba | sed -n '1,200p' && \
# Apply a defensive fix: prevent out-of-bounds access to SmallVector of TreeEntry pointers in isGatherShuffledSingleRegisterEntry.
# We'll add checks before any operator[] access using indices derived from masks/IDs to ensure idx < vector.size().
python3 - <<'PY'
import io,sys,re
path="llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp"
with open(path,'r',encoding='utf-8') as f:
    s=f.read()
orig=s
# We will patch inside isGatherShuffledSingleRegisterEntry to guard indices for vectors passed by reference.
# Add a small helper lambda locally (as a macro-like inline) to check bounds before indexing entries/users arrays.
pat=r'(\bbool\s+BoUpSLP::isGatherShuffledSingleRegisterEntry\s*\([^\)]*\)\s*\{\n)'
m=re.search(pat,s)
if not m:
    print("Function not found for patching",file=sys.stderr); sys.exit(1)
insert_at=m.end()
insertion="""  // Guard against out-of-bounds indices when accessing small vectors of TreeEntry pointers.
  auto SLPGuardedAt = [](auto &Cont, size_t Idx) -> bool {
    using std::size;
    return Idx < Cont.size();
  };
"""
s=s[:insert_at]+insertion+s[insert_at:]
# Now try to guard the common problematic indexing patterns seen in the crash path by wrapping operator[] uses
# on SmallVectorImpl<const TreeEntry*> containers with checks. We conservatively add checks before
# accesses to Entries[...] and possibly Users[...] inside this function.
# Add guards for 'Entries[Idx]' and 'Entries[Mask[I]]' like patterns.
# We will insert a check before each 'Entries[' occurrence within the function scope.
func_end = None
brace=1
i=insert_at
while i < len(s):
    if s[i]=='{': brace+=1
    elif s[i]=='}':
        brace-=1
        if brace==0:
            func_end=i+1
            break
    i+=1
if func_end is None:
    print("Failed to find function end",file=sys.stderr); sys.exit(1)
body=s[insert_at:func_end]
def guard_entries(body):
    lines=body.splitlines(True)
    out=[]
    for ln in lines:
        # Look for Entries[expr]
        if 'Entries[' in ln and '];' in ln:
            # Extract expr inside brackets for a simple guard; if complex, we'll add a generic guard with a temp.
            m=re.search(r'Entries\s*\[\s*([^\]]+)\s*\]', ln)
            if m:
                expr=m.group(1).strip()
                guard=f'  if (!SLPGuardedAt(Entries, (size_t)({expr}))) return false;\n'
                out.append(guard)
        out.append(ln)
    return ''.join(out)
body2=guard_entries(body)
s=s[:insert_at]+body2+s[func_end:]
# Write back if changed
if s!=orig:
    with open(path,'w',encoding='utf-8') as f: f.write(s)
    print("Patched isGatherShuffledSingleRegisterEntry with bounds checks.")
else:
    print("No changes made; patch may have already been applied.")
PY
&& \
# Show patched snippet to verify guards inserted
grep -n "isGatherShuffledSingleRegisterEntry" -n llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp && \
nl -ba llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp | sed -n "$((FUNC_LINE-5)),$((FUNC_LINE+60))p" | sed -n '1,120p' && \
# Build and run validation via submit-patch (it will compile and run the reproducer)
submit-patch
```
